explanation: Indicates that something is something else. Marks the topic and states what it is.
usageRules: Noun A + は + Noun B + です
exampleSentence: わたしはがくせいです。

explanation: Negative form indicating something is not something else.
usageRules: Noun A + は + Noun B + じゃないです / ではありません
exampleSentence: これはほんじゃないです。

explanation: Past tense affirmative. Indicates that something was something else.
usageRules: Noun A + は + Noun B + でした
exampleSentence: きのうはたいようびでした。

explanation: Past tense negative. Indicates that something was not something else.
usageRules: Noun A + は + Noun B + じゃなかったです / ではありませんでした
exampleSentence: きのうはやすみじゃなかったです。

explanation: Question form. Asks whether something is something else.
usageRules: Noun A + は + Noun B + ですか
exampleSentence: あなたはにほんじんですか。

explanation: Possessive or attributive particle. Links two nouns to show possession or relationship.
usageRules: Noun A + の + Noun B
exampleSentence: これはわたしのほんです。

explanation: Demonstrative determiners. この (this near me), その (that near you), あの (that over there).
usageRules: この/その/あの + Noun
exampleSentence: あのひとはだれですか。
exampleTranslation: Who is that person over there?

explanation: Demonstrative pronouns for places. ここ (here), そこ (there), あそこ (over there).
usageRules: ここ/そこ/あそこ + は + ...
exampleSentence: トイレはあそこです。

explanation: が marks the subject of a sentence while は marks the topic. Used for neutral description or when introducing new information.
usageRules: Topic + は + Subject + が + Predicate
exampleSentence: かれはあたまがいいです。
exampleTranslation: He is smart (lit. As for him, head is good).

explanation: を marks the direct object of an action verb.
usageRules: Noun + を + Verb
exampleSentence: りんごをたべます。

explanation: に indicates time or destination. Marks when something happens or where something goes.
usageRules: Time/Destination + に + Verb
exampleSentence: くじにがっこうにいきます。
exampleTranslation: I go to school at nine o'clock.

explanation: で indicates the location of an action or the means by which something is done.
usageRules: Location/Means + で + Verb
exampleSentence: こうえんでさんぽします。
exampleTranslation: I take a walk in the park.

explanation: へ indicates direction toward a place. Often interchangeable with に for destinations.
usageRules: Directional noun + へ + Verb of movement
exampleSentence: いえへかえります。

explanation: と connects nouns together (and) or marks the companion (with).
usageRules: Noun A + と + Noun B / Person + と + Verb
exampleSentence: ともだちとえいがをみます。
exampleTranslation: I watch a movie with my friend.

explanation: も means also or too. Replaces は, が, を when adding similar items.
usageRules: Noun + も + Predicate
exampleSentence: わたしもにほんごがすきです。

explanation: から marks the starting point (from) and まで marks the ending point (until).
usageRules: Time/Place + から + Time/Place + まで
exampleSentence: くじからごじまではたらきます。
exampleTranslation: I work from nine to five.

explanation: だけ means only or just. Limits the amount or extent.
usageRules: Noun/Quantifier + だけ
exampleSentence: これだけください。

explanation: Verbs of giving and receiving. あげる (give away), もらう (receive), くれる (give to me).
usageRules: Person + に + Object + を + あげる/もらう/くれる
exampleSentence: ともだちにプレゼントをあげました。
exampleTranslation: I gave a present to my friend.

explanation: Expresses desire to do something. Attaches to the masu-stem of a verb.
usageRules: Verb-masu-stem + たい
exampleSentence: にほんにいきたいです。
exampleTranslation: I want to go to Japan.

explanation: Expresses having done something before. Used with past experience.
usageRules: Verb-te-form + たことがある (past) / たことがない (negative)
exampleSentence: すしをたべたことがあります。

explanation: Indicates an ongoing action (progressive) or a resultant state.
usageRules: Verb-te-form + いる
exampleSentence: いまほんをよんでいます。
exampleTranslation: I am reading a book now.

explanation: A polite request. Please do something.
usageRules: Verb-te-form + ください
exampleSentence: まどをあけてください。

explanation: Asks or gives permission. May I do something / You may do something.
usageRules: Verb-te-form + も + いいです
exampleSentence: ここにすわってもいいですか。

explanation: Expresses prohibition. Must not do something.
usageRules: Verb-te-form + は + いけない / だめ
exampleSentence: ここでたべてはいけません。

explanation: Expresses obligation. Must do something / have to do something.
usageRules: Verb-nai-form + なければならない / なくてはいけない
exampleSentence: あしたはやくおきなければなりません。
exampleTranslation: I must wake up early tomorrow.

explanation: Indicates something is not necessary. Don't have to do something.
usageRules: Verb-nai-form + なくてもいい
exampleSentence: あしたこなくてもいいです。
exampleTranslation: You don't have to come tomorrow.

explanation: Gives advice or recommendation. Had better do something.
usageRules: Verb-ta-form + ほうがいい / Verb-nai-form + ない + ほうがいい
exampleSentence: くすりをのんだほうがいいです。

explanation: Indicates reason or cause. Because (more polite/softer than から).
usageRules: Sentence (plain) + ので + Result
exampleSentence: あめがふったので、いきませんでした。
exampleTranslation: Because it rained, I didn't go.

explanation: Indicates reason or cause. Because. More subjective than ので.
usageRules: Sentence (plain/polite) + から + Result
exampleSentence: おなかがすきましたから、たべましょう。

explanation: Connects two clauses with contrast (but) or simply leads into a topic.
usageRules: Sentence + が + Sentence
exampleSentence: にほんごはむずかしいですが、おもしろいです。
exampleTranslation: Japanese is difficult, but it's interesting.

explanation: Indicates a natural conditional. When/if A happens, B naturally follows.
usageRules: Sentence (plain, non-past) + と + Result
exampleSentence: はるになると、さくらがさきます。
exampleTranslation: When spring comes, cherry blossoms bloom.

explanation: Indicates the time when something happens. When.
usageRules: Verb (plain) / Adjective + とき + Main clause
exampleSentence: にほんにいくとき、パスポートがいります。
exampleTranslation: When going to Japan, you need a passport.

explanation: Expresses doing something before another action. Before.
usageRules: Verb (dictionary form) / Noun + の + まえに
exampleSentence: たべるまえに、てをあらいます。
exampleTranslation: I wash my hands before eating.

explanation: Expresses doing something after another action. After.
usageRules: Verb-ta-form / Noun + の + あとに
exampleSentence: しごとのあとに、ジムにいきます。
exampleTranslation: I go to the gym after work.

explanation: Indicates that one action happens after another. After doing.
usageRules: Verb-te-form + から
exampleSentence: てをあらってから、たべます。

explanation: Lists representative activities. Do things like A and B (among others).
usageRules: Verb-ta-form + り + Verb-ta-form + り + する
exampleSentence: にちようびはほんをよんだり、さんぽしたりします。
exampleTranslation: On Sundays I do things like reading books and taking walks.

explanation: Indicates a change of state. Become (something).
usageRules: い-Adjective (remove い) + く + なる / な-Adjective + に + なる / Noun + に + なる
exampleSentence: さむくなりました。

explanation: Indicates making something a certain way or deciding on something.
usageRules: い-Adjective (remove い) + く + する / な-Adjective + に + する / Noun + に + する
exampleSentence: へやをきれいにします。
exampleTranslation: I will clean the room (make it clean).

explanation: Indicates doing something excessively. Too much.
usageRules: Verb-masu-stem + すぎる / い-Adjective (remove い) + すぎる / な-Adjective + すぎる
exampleSentence: たべすぎました。

explanation: Indicates ease or difficulty of an action. Easy/difficult to do.
usageRules: Verb-masu-stem + やすい / Verb-masu-stem + にくい
exampleSentence: このじてんしゃはのりやすいです。
exampleTranslation: This bicycle is easy to ride.

explanation: Volitional form. Let's do something. Offers a suggestion or invitation.
usageRules: Verb-masu-stem + ましょう
exampleSentence: いっしょにこうえんにいきましょう。
exampleTranslation: Let's go to the park together.

explanation: Polite invitation. Won't you do something? More tentative than ましょう.
usageRules: Verb-masu-stem + ませんか
exampleSentence: いっしょにごはんをたべませんか。

explanation: Offering to do something for someone. Shall I do something?
usageRules: Verb-masu-stem + ましょうか
exampleSentence: まどをしめましょうか。

explanation: A polite request for an item. Please give me something.
usageRules: Noun + をください
exampleSentence: みずをください。

explanation: A polite request for a service or item. Please handle this / I'd like this.
usageRules: Noun + をおねがいします
exampleSentence: コーヒーをおねがいします。

explanation: Asks if someone knows something. Do you know?
usageRules: Noun + を + しっていますか / しっています (know) / しりません (don't know)
exampleSentence: あのひとをしっていますか。

explanation: Expresses desire to possess something. Want something.
usageRules: Noun + が + ほしい
exampleSentence: あたらしいけいたいがほしいです。

explanation: Expresses an opinion or thought. I think that...
usageRules: Sentence (plain) + と思う / と思います
exampleSentence: あしたはあめだとおもいます。
exampleTranslation: I think it will rain tomorrow.

explanation: Quotes what someone said. Someone said that...
usageRules: Quote (plain) + と + 言う / 言いました
exampleSentence: かのじょはあしたくるといいました。
exampleTranslation: She said she will come tomorrow.

explanation: Adds explanatory or emphatic tone. It is that... / The reason is...
usageRules: Sentence (plain) + のです / んです (more conversational)
exampleSentence: あたまがいたいんです。
exampleTranslation: It's that I have a headache (explaining why I look unwell).

explanation: Nominalization. Turns a verb or adjective into a noun phrase.
usageRules: Verb (plain) + の/こと + Particle
exampleSentence: ほんをよむのがすきです。

explanation: Expresses ability. Can do something / be able to do something.
usageRules: Verb (dictionary form) + ことができる
exampleSentence: にほんごをはなすことができます。

explanation: A polite request asking someone to do something for you. Will you do for me?
usageRules: Verb-te-form + くれますか
exampleSentence: てつだってくれますか。

explanation: Asks for permission to do something. Please let me do something.
usageRules: Verb-causative-form + て + ください
exampleSentence: じゆうにさせてください。
exampleTranslation: Please let me do as I like.

explanation: A command or instruction, softer than imperative. Do something.
usageRules: Verb-masu-stem + なさい
exampleSentence: しゅくだいをしなさい。

explanation: Expresses probability or conjecture. Probably / I think.
usageRules: Sentence (plain) + でしょう
exampleSentence: あしたもあめでしょう。
exampleTranslation: It will probably rain tomorrow too.

explanation: Expresses possibility. Might / may / perhaps.
usageRules: Sentence (plain) + かもしれない
exampleSentence: かれはくるかもしれません。

explanation: Expresses something expected based on information. Should be / supposed to be.
usageRules: Sentence (plain) + はずです / はずがない (negative)
exampleSentence: かれはしっているはずです。

explanation: Expresses intention. Intend to do something / plan to do something.
usageRules: Verb (dictionary form) + つもりです / Verb-nai-form + つもりです
exampleSentence: にほんにりゅうがくするつもりです。
exampleTranslation: I intend to study abroad in Japan.

explanation: Expresses a wish or desire politely. I think I want to / I would like to.
usageRules: Verb-masu-stem + たい + と思います
exampleSentence: にほんごをべんきょうしたいとおもいます。
exampleTranslation: I think I want to study Japanese.

explanation: Expresses even if / although. Concessive clause.
usageRules: Verb-te-form + も + Clause / い-Adjective-te-form + も
exampleSentence: あめがふっても、いきます。
exampleTranslation: Even if it rains, I will go.

explanation: Expresses limitation. Only / nothing but. Used with negative verb.
usageRules: Noun + しか + Negative verb
exampleSentence: ごひゃくえんしかもっていません。

explanation: Used for comparisons. Than.
usageRules: Noun A + より + Noun B + のほうが + Adjective / Noun A + より + Adjective
exampleSentence: にほんごよりえいごのほうがかんたんです。

explanation: Indicates that one thing is more than another. More... than...
usageRules: Noun A + より + Noun B + のほうが + Adjective
exampleSentence: くるまよりじてんしゃのほうがはやいです。
exampleTranslation: The bicycle is faster than the car.

explanation: Asking which one is more between two items. Which is more?
usageRules: Noun A + と + Noun B + と + どちらが + Adjective + か
exampleSentence: ねこといぬとどちらがすきですか。
exampleTranslation: Which do you like more, cats or dogs?

explanation: Generic counter for things (up to 10). Used for small objects and abstract items.
usageRules: Number + つ
exampleSentence: りんごをみっつください。

explanation: Counter for people.
usageRules: Number (special readings) + 人 (にん)
exampleSentence: かぞくはよにんです。

explanation: Counter for flat objects such as paper, plates, stamps, etc.
usageRules: Number + 枚 (まい)
exampleSentence: かみをごまいください。
exampleTranslation: Please give me five sheets of paper.

explanation: Counter for small animals, fish, insects.
usageRules: Number + 匹 (ひき/ぴき/びき)
exampleSentence: ねこがにひきいます。

explanation: Used with a negative verb to mean not very / not much.
usageRules: あまり + Negative verb/adjective
exampleSentence: さしみはあまりすきじゃないです。
exampleTranslation: I don't like sashimi very much.

explanation: Indicates an unexpectedly large (or small) quantity. As many as / as few as.
usageRules: Quantifier + も + Verb (affirmative for large amount, negative for small amount)
exampleSentence: ともだちがじゅうにんもきました。
exampleTranslation: As many as ten friends came.

explanation: Presents alternatives. Whether A or B.
usageRules: Sentence A (plain) + か + Sentence B (plain) + か + Predicate
exampleSentence: いくかかないか、まだわかりません。
exampleTranslation: I don't know yet whether I'll go or not.

explanation: Indefinite pronoun. Someone (だれか), somewhere (どこか), something (なにか).
usageRules: Question word (だれ/どこ/なに) + か + Particle/Verb
exampleSentence: どこかにかぎをおとしました。

explanation: Negative universal. No one (だれも〜ない), nowhere (どこも〜ない), nothing (なにも〜ない).
usageRules: Question word + も + Negative verb
exampleSentence: きょうしつだれもいません。
exampleTranslation: There is no one in the classroom.

explanation: Universal affirmative. Everyone (だれも), everywhere (どこも), everything (なんでも).
usageRules: Question word + も + Affirmative verb
exampleSentence: かれはなんでもたべます。

explanation: Asking for opinion or suggestion. How about / what do you think of?
usageRules: Noun + はどうですか
exampleSentence: このレストランはどうですか。

explanation: Expresses liking something. Like something.
usageRules: Noun + が + 好き (すき) + です
exampleSentence: わたしはくるまがすきです。

explanation: Expresses skill level. Good at / bad at something.
usageRules: Noun + が + 上手 (じょうず) / 下手 (へた) + です
exampleSentence: かのじょはりょうりがじょうずです。

explanation: Expresses going/coming somewhere in order to do something.
usageRules: Verb-masu-stem + に + いく / くる
exampleSentence: ほんをかりに、としょかんにいきます。
exampleTranslation: I'm going to the library to borrow a book.

explanation: Indicates beginning of an action. Start doing something.
usageRules: Verb-masu-stem + はじめる
exampleSentence: にほんごのべんきょうをはじめました。

explanation: Indicates starting or beginning an action suddenly. Start / begin to.
usageRules: Verb-masu-stem + だす
exampleSentence: こどもがないだしました。

explanation: Indicates continuing an action. Continue doing something.
usageRules: Verb-masu-stem + つづける
exampleSentence: いちじかんあるきつづけました。
exampleTranslation: I continued walking for one hour.

explanation: Indicates completion of an action. Finish doing something.
usageRules: Verb-masu-stem + おわる
exampleSentence: しゅくだいをおわらせました。

explanation: Polite expression of wanting to do something.
usageRules: Verb-masu-stem + たいです
exampleSentence: いまはごはんをたべたいです。

explanation: Expresses obligation. Must do / have to do (alternative form).
usageRules: Verb-nai-form (remove ない) + くてはいけない
exampleSentence: くすりをのまなくてはいけません。

explanation: Existence. がいる for living things, がある for inanimate objects.
usageRules: Place + に + Living thing + がいる / Thing + がある
exampleSentence: つくえのうえにほんがあります。
exampleTranslation: There is a book on the desk.

explanation: Expresses riding or boarding a vehicle.
usageRules: Vehicle + に + のる
exampleSentence: でんしゃにのります。

explanation: Expresses getting off a vehicle.
usageRules: Vehicle + を + おりる
exampleSentence: バスをおりました。

explanation: Superlative comparison. Among X, Y is the most.
usageRules: Group + のなかで + Subject + が + いちばん + Adjective
exampleSentence: このなかでどれがいちばんたかいですか。
exampleTranslation: Among these, which is the most expensive?

explanation: Listing items with や implies a non-exhaustive list (and other things).
usageRules: Noun A + や + Noun B + など + Particle
exampleSentence: くるまやバイクなどがあります。
exampleTranslation: There are things like cars and motorcycles.
